original_hypothesis.title: ETH_RUNE transferTo Uses tx.origin Enabling Token Theft via Intermediary Contracts
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: ETH_RUNE's transferTo function uses tx.origin instead of msg.sender, allowing any contract in the call chain to transfer tokens from the original transaction signer without their explicit consent or approval. A malicious contract that a user interacts with for any reason can silently drain their entire ETH.RUNE balance.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: This is a well-documented anti-pattern (SWC-115). The code comment acknowledges the risk but dismisses it as equivalent to infinite approvals. However, unlike infinite approvals, this requires zero user consent - any interaction with any malicious contract suffices. The documentation says ETH.RUNE is deprecated and out of scope, but the code is in the auditable contracts list.

proof_of_signal.pattern: tx.origin used as source of funds instead of msg.sender

preconditions: User interacts with any contract (even unrelated to RUNE)

initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: Any intermediary contract can drain user's RUNE without approval
initial_code_evidence.missing_safeguards: Should use msg.sender or require explicit approval

analysis_questions: How many users still hold ETH.RUNE?
Is ETH.RUNE used in any active protocol flows?
validation_requirements: Deploy malicious contract that calls transferTo during normal interaction
Confirm victim's RUNE is transferred without their knowledge

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: Attacker deploys a DeFi contract. User calls any function on it. The contract calls ETH_RUNE.transferTo(attackerAddress, victimBalance), draining all the user's ETH.RUNE tokens.

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: Complete drainage of victim's ETH.RUNE balance
potential_impact_if_confirmed.affected_users: Any ETH.RUNE holder who interacts with malicious contracts

original_hypothesis.test_coverage_gap: No test for transferTo exploitation via intermediary contract

technical_investigation_focus.detection_analysis: Visible as transferTo call from non-user address

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
